test_description: For each masked temperature map, fit and subtract the monopole and dipole on the unmasked pixels, set masked pixels to zero, and compute the masked pseudo-spectrum \tilde C_l up to lmax=30. Define f_sky2 = mean(W^2) for the applied mask W, and compute the low-l cut-sky power P_low = [1/(4*pi*f_sky2)] * sum_{l=2}^{30} (2l+1) \tilde C_l. The anomaly statistic is the single scalar S = ln(P_low), in log microK^2 units. Compare the Planck SMICA value with the same scalar from the 1000 LCDM simulations, using the lower tail as the power-deficit anomaly direction.
test_hypothesis: The Planck SMICA masked temperature map has anomalously low low-ell power, i.e. a smaller S = ln(P_low) than expected from LCDM simulations.
justificaton: The statistic is explicitly designed to detect a low-power deficit, and the code defines the anomaly direction as the lower tail, where smaller S means lower low-ell power.

custom_summary.test_description: Masked Low-l Power Deficit Log-Amplitude: for each masked temperature map, remove the best-fit monopole and dipole on unmasked pixels, set masked pixels to zero, compute the cut-sky pseudo-spectrum through ell=30, and return S = ln(P_low) for P_low = (4*pi*f_sky2)^-1 sum_{ell=2}^{30} (2ell+1) Ctilde_ell. The lower tail is the low-power-deficit anomaly direction.
custom_summary.statistic: S = ln(P_low), where P_low is the f_sky2-corrected cut-sky pseudo-spectrum power summed over 2 <= ell <= 30
custom_summary.tail_of_interest: lower tail (smaller S means lower low-ell power)
custom_summary.mask_normalization: f_sky2 = mean(W^2), with the supplied finite-pixel mask treated as binary W=1 unmasked and W=0 masked
custom_summary.monopole_dipole_treatment: best-fit monopole plus Cartesian dipole removed on unmasked pixels before zero-filling the mask and computing the pseudo-spectrum

plot_spec.title: Masked low-ell power log-amplitude
plot_spec.caption: Distribution of log cut-sky low-ell power from the LCDM simulations. The low-power-deficit anomaly corresponds to the lower tail.

custom_summary.interpretation: Planck is in the lowest 5% of simulations for masked low-ell power, indicating a low-power deficit by this statistic.

test_summary.Description: Monopole/dipole-subtracted masked SMICA and LCDM maps were zero-filled in the mask, converted to pseudo-\(C_\ell\) up to \(\ell=30\), and summarized by \(S=\ln(P_{\rm low})\), with the lower tail testing low power.
test_summary.Results: Planck has \(S=7.553\) (\(P_{\rm low}=1906\,\mu{\rm K}^2\)); simulations have mean \(7.786\), median \(7.780\), and std \(0.146\). Only 42/1000 simulations are lower, giving one-tailed \(p=0.042\) (\(\approx1.7\sigma\) one-sided).
test_summary.Interpretation: The Planck value lies in the lowest 4.2% of the LCDM simulations, supporting a low-\(\ell\) power deficit, but the significance is marginal and below a robust 2-sigma anomaly threshold.
test_summary.Comparison to literature: Broad low-\(\ell\) temperature power deficits are well known from WMAP/Planck anomaly studies, including Planck Collaboration, “Planck 2018 results. VII. Isotropy and Statistics of the CMB” (A&A 641, A7; arXiv:1906.02552). However, no close literature match was identified for this exact masked log-amplitude pseudo-spectrum statistic.
